# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent cicd security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build Workflow

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      build_commands:
        description: 'Custom build commands'
        required: true
        default: 'echo "No build commands provided"'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - name: Checkout code
      uses: actions/checkout@v3

    - name: Validate build commands
      id: validate
      run: |
        if [[ "${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}" =~ [^a-zA-Z0-9\ \-\_\.\;] ]]; then
          echo "Invalid characters detected in build commands."
          exit 1
        fi

    - name: Execute build commands
      if: steps.validate.outcome == 'success'
      run: |
        set -e
        IFS=';' read -ra COMMANDS <<< "${{ github.event.inputs.build_commands }}"
        for cmd in "${COMMANDS[@]}"; do
          echo "Executing: $cmd"
          eval "$cmd"
        done
      shell: bash

    - name: Clean up
      if: always()
      run: |
        echo "Cleaning up..."
        # Add any cleanup commands here

    - name: Post-build actions
      if: success()
      run: |
        echo "Build completed successfully."
        # Add any post-build actions here
